items.title: How to score a deal on Grand Theft Auto 6 pre-orders tonight
items.summary:
Update: Grand Theft Auto 6 pre-orders are now live at Amazon on PS5 and Xbox. More details below on scoring a gift card deal on pre-orders.

Amazon’s Prime Day sale may be in full swing, but Rockstar grabbed plenty of attention away from the deals today with fresh GTA 6 pre-order details. The company has officially confirmed a $100 Ultimate Edition, putting it $20 above the standard version’s $80 price tag. You can, however, grab a $100 PlayStation Store gift card from Newegg right now for $88 with promo code FTTF6933, effectively bringing the PS5 Ultimate Edition down to $88 – just $8 more than the standard version. Head below for a closer look at what each edition includes.

items.title: Grand Theft Auto 6 pre-orders now live!
items.summary:
Update: Grand Theft Auto 6 pre-orders are now live at Amazon on PS5 and Xbox.

Okay here we go! Grand Theft Auto 6 pre-orders are now live. Retailers like Amazon, Walmart, GameStop, and others are at the ready to take orders on boxed copies and you won’t have to pay until later this year when it launches in November. All digital copies are expected to be live on the PS and Xbox storefronts tonight as well.

items.title: Pebblebee Halo is an Apple Find My/Android Find Hub tracker, flashlight, and safety siren, all for just $40 this Prime Day
items.summary:
Pebblebee Halo Apple Find My/Android Find Hub tracker

Buy for $40 (Reg. $60)

Prime-exclusive discount.

What we love

Pebblebee’s Halo stands out by working with both Apple Find My and Android Find Hub, so it is not tied to one platform. It is a compact, USB-C rechargeable unit with a carabiner clip, but it also doubles as a flashlight and personal safety alarm. You can pull off its magnetic cap to trigger a 130dB siren and a 150-lumen flashing strobe, and the Pebblebee app can also share your live location with trusted contacts.

items.summary: Prime Day is here, and it is a good excuse to grab the Switch 2 accessories you have been putting off. There are deals on just about everything from 8BitDo controllers and PowerA headsets to JSAUX charging gear, tomtoc cases, and sling bags to keep your handheld protected on the go. Prices start at just $7 right now, with savings of up to 50% across the collection. Whether you need a better way to carry it around, an extra controller for couch co-op, or just a charger that is easier to travel with, we have rounded up some of our favorite Switch 2 accessory deals worth checking out below.

We also have the Nothing CMF Buds 2 at just $24 (50% off) – everything awaits right here, and you’ll find a breakdown of the best deals below.

The sale covers plenty of practical everyday gear, including MagSafe and Qi2 power banks, wall chargers, USB-C cables, audio gear, and more. The deals on the items included in the sale start at just $6, so you don’t necessarily have to spend a ton to refresh a travel charging kit, pick up a backup battery pack, or simply stock up on cables.
